__doc__: Allow-list for the Layer-1 geometry audit (scripts/gallery-geometry-audit.mjs). Each entry excuses a HIGH-severity finding that is a known, intentional, or unfixable-by-design pattern; the finding is still REPORTED (marked `allowed`) but does not fail `--gate`. Only HIGH findings gate, so this list only needs entries for HIGH classes (A1 real zero-gap, B3 page overflow, I4 modal-out-of-viewport, L1/L5 content-rendering, B1 genuine flex-WRAP-with-room). MEDIUM/LOW classes are review signal and never gate. Match fields: class (required), surface ('*' = any), selector (substring, optional), viewport (optional). Keep every reason specific + defensible — an allow-list entry is an accepted defect, not a silenced detector.

entries.reason: Intentional known-positive fixture (DefectRepro.tsx) reproducing the original hardware Disconnected/Connect zero-gap (user miss #1). The detector MUST flag it — that is the fixture's purpose — so it is allow-listed for --gate but still reported as acceptance proof.
Intentional B1 known-positive fixture (DefectRepro.tsx, user miss #2/#3: premature wrap). The detector MUST flag it (detector-acceptance asserts it FIRES); allow-listed for --gate, still reported.

Intentional L1 known-positive fixture (DefectRepro.tsx, user miss #11a: raw TeX with no KaTeX). The detector MUST flag it (detector-acceptance asserts it FIRES); allow-listed for --gate, still reported.

The composer's `toolbar_status` chip strip (ChatInput.tsx: `flex flex-wrap items-center gap-1.5`, testid `composer-status-slot`) is an intentional variable-length wrap row holding the MCP / assistant / memory / summary chips. On a 272px mobile composer two chips near-fit and wrap by a hair (~23px slack). Wrapping is the CORRECT behaviour for a chip row whose length depends on how many tools/pills are active — the only alternative (flex-nowrap) would clip chips off the edge. The row renders on whichever deep-chat surface has ≥2 chips (surface-variable across runs), so this is scoped to the stable testid, not a surface. Per ROUND3_FIX_SPEC Shard 1: allow-list the status-chip row (do NOT force it nowrap). Still reported; excused from --gate.
